task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution meets all the criteria and correctly handles an empty list as an edge case.
trace:
**

I chose to sort the list first to simplify finding the median. For an odd-length list, I returned the middle element directly as a float. For an even-length list, I calculated the average of the two middle elements and returned it. The edge case of an empty list returns `None`, which is handled at the beginning of the function. This approach ensures that the code is both concise and readable while correctly addressing all specified criteria.
